scope.summary: Implement topstitch polygon count optimization that collapses near-degenerate triangles generated by topstitch edge insertion before export, maintaining a maximum edge-length ratio threshold and emitting a TailorTopstitchOptimized event
acceptance_criteria: optimize_topstitch_polygons(mesh: &mut SolverMesh, max_aspect_ratio: f32) collapses triangles whose longest-to-shortest-edge ratio exceeds max_aspect_ratio (default 8.0) in the vicinity of topstitch edge insertions
The optimization pass runs after simulation and before export; it does not run during simulation substeps
UV coordinates are preserved for all non-collapsed vertices; collapsed vertices interpolate their UV from the surviving edge endpoint
The total triangle count in the vicinity of each topstitch path reduces by at least 20% for paths with particle distance below 3mm
An advisory ValidationDescriptor TOPSTITCH_POLYGON_DENSITY warns when a topstitch path introduces triangles with aspect ratio > 8.0 before optimization is run
scope.risk_if_missed: Topstitch decoration is a standard garment detailing feature. Without polygon optimization, topstitch insertions create mesh density spikes (hundreds of tiny triangles per cm) that inflate exported file size, slow DCC tool import, and produce subdivision artifacts around topstitch seams.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
